chart_type: Multi-series line plot with markers at integer years

y_axis.label: GDP per capita (international $, 2021 prices)
y_axis.parsing: Strip leading '$' and thousands ',', store value as integer dollar amount.

phase4_artifact_check.monotonicity_violations_explained: UK has a 24% range jump representing the 2020 COVID-era contraction and rebound, also visible in the source image; not an extraction artifact.

known_caveats: Y values are pixel-extracted: about +/- $200 expected error per point (1 px = $497).

Label-occluded segments (e.g. behind country name labels near the right edge) were interpolated linearly from adjacent in-frame points; the exact interpolation spans are recorded in extraction_info.json.
First and last year-label centers (1990, 2024) are anchored inside the axis frame in the source; calibration uses the middle six year-label centers (1995-2020) to avoid that bias.
Use the original World Bank / Eurostat / OECD / IMF source data for anything beyond rough comparison.
